name: La Sènia - Alcanar - Ermita del Remei - Castillo de Ulldecona – return through the Montsià
name_original: La Sènia - Alcanar - Ermita del Remei - Castillo de Ulldecona – vuelta por el Montsià
region_intro: Road cycling routes around La Sénia traverse a diverse landscape, from flat plains adorned with ancient olive trees to challenging mountainous ascents. The region serves as a gateway to Els Ports Natural Park, offering demanding routes with significant elevation gains. Cyclists can also find less strenuous rides through agricultural plains and along the Sénia river, which marks the border between Catalonia and the Valencian Community. This varied terrain provides options for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around La Sénia The most popular road cycling route is La Sènia - Alcanar - Ermita del Remei - Castillo de Ulldecona – return through the Montsià, a 43.8 miles (70.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around Ulldecona and the Montsià region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ulldecona Reservoir – Vallibona loop from la Sénia, a difficult 69.4 miles (111.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and offers views of the Ulldecona Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Tortosa Municipal Market loop from la Sénia, a 47.0 miles (75.7 km) trail leading through the plains towards Tortosa, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around La Sénia is defined by mountainous terrain within Els Ports Natural Park, extensive olive groves, and flatter routes towards the Ebro Delta. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult ascents. The routes in La Sénia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore La Sénia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around La Sénia?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around La Sénia listed on komoot, offering options for various skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in La Sénia?
A: La Sénia offers a diverse landscape for road cycling. You'll find challenging mountainous ascents within Els Ports Natural Park, featuring significant elevation gains. For less strenuous rides, there are flatter routes through agricultural plains with ancient olive trees and along the Sénia river, or even towards the Ebro Delta.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in La Sénia?
A: Yes, experienced cyclists will find demanding routes, particularly within Els Ports Natural Park. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir – Vallibona loop from la Sénia&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 111.7 km path with significant elevation gain. The ascent to Mont Caro, an 18 km climb with an average gradient of 7.4%, is another highlight for serious climbers in the region.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly road cycling routes in La Sénia?
A: While many routes offer moderate to difficult challenges, the region also provides options for less strenuous rides. The plains around the Sénia river, characterized by ancient olive trees, offer picturesque and gentler routes. The Ebro Delta, close enough to La Sénia, also features completely flat routes, such as a 110 km loop through the National Park with minimal elevation gain, suitable for a more relaxed pace.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes incorporate scenic natural features. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, known for its changing water colors. The region is also famous for its 'immense sea of olive trees,' some of which are ancient and monumental. For stunning views, routes may pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/688369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyagalera Summit (1,035 m)&lt;/a&gt; or the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around La Sénia are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966570356/la-senia-alcanar-ermita-del-remei-castillo-de-ulldecona-return-through-the-montsia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sènia - Alcanar - Ermita del Remei - Castillo de Ulldecona – return through the Montsià&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route. Another example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38234381" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tortosa Municipal Market loop from la Sénia&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the plains.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in La Sénia?
A: The diverse climate of La Sénia makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling. While the region can be warm in summer, early morning rides are still enjoyable. Winter cycling is also possible, especially on lower elevation routes, though conditions in the mountainous areas of Els Ports Natural Park may require more preparation.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling in Els Ports Natural Park?
A: While the region is very cycling-friendly, it's always advisable to check with local authorities or the park's official website for any specific regulations or permits, especially for certain protected areas or during specific seasons. You can find more information about the region's natural attractions on &lt;a href="https://www.visitalasenia.es/discover/nature-tourism/7/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitalasenia.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in La Sénia?
A: The road cycling routes in La Sénia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain climbs to scenic routes through ancient olive groves, and the beautiful natural features like the Ulldecona Reservoir.

Q: Are there options for bike-friendly accommodation or services in the area?
A: La Sénia is increasingly recognized as a cycling-friendly destination. Some establishments in the broader 'Grupo Senia' offer cycling stays, which may include amenities like bike rentals and recommendations for routes. It's recommended to check directly with local accommodations for specific bike-friendly services.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with other outdoor activities in La Sénia?
A: Absolutely. Beyond cycling, La Sénia is excellent for various outdoor pursuits. You can explore numerous itineraries for hiking and trail running, or visit attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region's natural parks and river areas provide ample opportunities for diverse outdoor experiences.

Q: Are there routes that pass through the Maestrat region?
A: Yes, cyclists can explore the Maestrat region, which features routes with smaller climbs like Valltorta or Bandereta. The area also hosts cycling events such as the 'Marcha Cicloturista Desafio Maestrat' for road cyclists, indicating its popularity for cycling.
tips: Excellent viewpoint of Les Cases d'Alcanar and Punta de la Banya in the Ebro Delta.

The Remei hermitage is located in a sensational natural and landscape environment, with beautiful panoramic views of the Alcanar plain, the Alfacs bay and the Ebro Delta. The complex, made up of a hermitage and a guesthouse, was built in two different times. The oldest nave is in the Gothic style, while the extension to the building and its characteristic dome are in the Baroque style.

Fortification that stands on the Puig del Castell, in the Serra Grossa. For a long time, this building has been a strategic area for its control of the territory with great visibility thanks to its location 256 meters above sea level.

waypoints.name: View of Les Cases d'Alcanar and Punta de la Banya, Delta del Ebro
